reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — headless-Chrome load+run generation harness + desktop+mobile+light/dark screenshots of all 5 pages

rubric.evidence: Load+run harness in headless Chrome (fresh profile, served copy) built the real text-generation pipeline (onnx-community/Apertus-v1.1-1.5B-Instruct-QAD-INT4-ONNX, device:'wasm', dtype:'q4') on Transformers.js 4.2.0 and generated (greedy, max_new_tokens 40) for 'the capital of France': OUTPUT = 'The capital of France is Paris. It is famous for its rich history, culture, and iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, Notre-Dame Cathedral, and the Louvre Museum.' Coherent, factual, non-degenerate. Worker (worker.js) runs the pipeline + TextStreamer off the main thread.
rubric.notes: Genuinely distinct family (Apertus / Swiss AI Initiative), NOT a relabel. The apertus model class is ABSENT from the shared 3.7.5 pin (verified: loading on 3.7.5 throws 'Unsupported model type: apertus'); the worker pins 4.2.0 LOCALLY (isolated escape hatch, sam2 precedent). INT4-QAD build avoids the degenerate-q4 trap — the quantization-aware weights generate cleanly.

rubric.evidence: Output is a coherent chat continuation conditioned on the prompt. 'See inside' renders the exact constructed chat template + the real top-k next-token distribution (12 rows) from one forward pass. Practical page runs JSON.parse() on the model output to prove machine-readable shape; wild page steers the answer language; multi-model page shows Qwen3-Embedding cosine scores + Apertus's grounded answer.
rubric.notes: Expected shape (streamed tokens; top-k probabilities) is correct and legibly surfaced.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (linked to card + swiss-ai base), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend (WebAssembly/CPU), declares-quantisation (q4), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance lists model, base (Swiss AI/SNAI), architecture (Apertus decoder, xIELU), params/dtype 'q4 (onnx/model_q4.onnx)', backend 'WebAssembly (CPU) — no WebGPU required', runtime 'Transformers.js 4.2.0 (pinned in this worker)', ~1.76 GB, multilingual, Apache-2.0. The 4.2.0 pin + INT4 build are explicitly disclosed in the copy.
rubric.notes: Honest disclosure including the isolated version pin and the true ~1.76 GB size.

rubric.evidence: Uses the shared createModelLoader (lib/model-loader.js), which is version-agnostic (scans Cache Storage by modelId) so auto-init works despite the 4.2.0 worker pin. Fresh profile → honest absent state (Download ~1.76 GB) with no silent transfer; a valid current cached copy auto-inits with only a Clear-cached-model control. requiresWebGPU:false.
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract; the lede explicitly warns about the ~1.76 GB one-time download so a large transfer is never a surprise.

rubric.evidence: role=status aria-live=polite regions on main + every use-case page; 'Generating on the CPU…' → 'Done.'; failures set a readable .err status. Stop button interrupts generation (InterruptableStoppingCriteria). Loader surfaces checking→initialising→ready and Retry on failure. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: WASM path, no adapter gate.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error/stop wired accessibly. Explicit partial/evicted and offline-cached cache-state drives were not separately exercised this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main: system-prompt textarea, message textarea, Send/Stop, prompt chips, sampling controls (temp/top_p ranges default 0.8/0.9, max-tokens number, greedy checkbox that disables temp/top_p), Clear, and See-inside 'Show next-token distribution'. Basics: input + Ask + chips. Practical: sample chips + textarea + Extract. Wild: language <select> + chips + chat. Multi-model: two loaders + KB textarea + question chips + Retrieve&answer. 27 focusable controls; Send/textarea >=44px, chips >=36px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced via click; a full keyboard-only traversal is the remaining verification. Fixed a mobile inline-code overflow during this pass.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional assistant message and answer areas show muted placeholders; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile. The verified output was produced by the on-device run (observed via the harness).

conformance: buttons-have-names (all buttons carry text), images-have-alt (no content ). Status/transcript/answer regions role=status/aria-live=polite; labelled textareas + language select; chip groups aria-label; greedy checkbox toggles temp/top_p disabled state.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline; a screen-reader pass over the streaming transcript and top-k grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix: scrollWidth-clientWidth==0 at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages after fixing a 23px mobile overflow from a long inline <code> (added p code{overflow-wrap:anywhere}). 0 console errors, 0 failed requests. Light+dark screenshots read against the design system.
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on this driven pass; the overflow fix is the one in-place change made during review.

rubric.evidence: All generation + the top-k forward pass run in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the CPU-bound work never blocks the main thread; tokens stream via postMessage/TextStreamer. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture).
rubric.notes: A 1.5B INT4 model on WASM/CPU is honestly slow (a few tok/s) and heavy (~1.76 GB) — the page discloses both. An explicit INP measurement while streaming would confirm the off-thread mitigation; the size/latency cost is the trade for a fully-open, auditable model on plain CPU.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream text token by token without long tasks INP
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread and stream incrementally so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation and the top-k forward pass run in models/apertus-1-5b/worker.js; tokens are posted to the UI incrementally via TextStreamer callbacks; the main thread only appends text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run coherent; console clean (0 errors)

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep critical interaction work ahead of deferrable background work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generation is dispatched to the worker on explicit Send; the Stop button posts an interrupt so the user can reclaim control mid-generation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap/gap/intrinsic sizing; set overflow-wrap/min-inline-size:0 on long unbreakable content; wrap wide code in scroll containers.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; glance cells + inline <code> overflow-wrap:anywhere (fix); <pre> code scrolls. Verified 0px overflow at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check clean; driven overflow==0 after the inline-code fix; screenshots read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status; transcript/answer aria-live=polite; labelled textareas + language select; chips grouped with aria-label.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); all pages link the shared stylesheet + crumb back.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and does Enter-to-send / Shift+Enter behave on a real device?
On a low-memory mobile device, does the ~1.76 GB q4 download + WASM runtime stay within budget, or should the page hard-warn / offer a smaller sibling before download?
Drive the partial/evicted and offline-cached cache states explicitly (delete a cached file → Re-download; offline warm cache → still runs) — important given the large multi-file external-data build.
Measure real INP and tok/s on a mid-range mobile CPU while streaming, and confirm the 4.2.0-pinned worker caches identically to 3.7.5 pages under the shared loader.
Verify multilingual output quality on non-Latin scripts (the base claims 1000+ languages) beyond the Latin examples driven here.

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for apertus-1-5b across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of streamed tokens and the top-k grid).
Add a pre-download memory/size gate for the ~1.76 GB build on constrained devices, linking the smaller WASM LLM demos (qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm, ernie, lfm2) as lighter alternatives.
Add a WebGPU fast-path (q4f16) alternative page for Apertus with an honest adapter probe, comparing tok/s vs the WASM q4 path on-device.

summary: apertus-1-5b is a genuinely distinct, honest demo of the Swiss AI Initiative's fully-open, compliant, multilingual Apertus family (v1.1 1.5B, INT4-QAD). Verified with the exact ONNX build in headless Chrome on the isolated Transformers.js 4.2.0 worker pin (the apertus class is absent from the shared 3.7.5): coherent non-degenerate generation, off-main-thread streaming, honest absent→Download (~1.76 GB, disclosed) / cached→auto-init loader states, and 0px horizontal overflow + 0 console errors at 360x740 and 1280x800 across all five pages after fixing one inline-code mobile overflow. Support recorded ok/ok. Remaining work: keyboard/SR traversal, a pre-download size/memory gate for the large build, and explicit cache-state drives. Revision 1.
